# W22 Probe AG — Q-velocity sensitivity (synthetic KF experiment)

*Auto-generated by `experiments/analyze_probe_ag.py`.*

## Question

Probe AC+AF found that production KF has |lag-1 autocorrelation| ≈ 0.5 on risk
innovations (MODERATE/borderline MIS-TUNED), with root cause traced to
`P_next = F @ P @ F.T` (no Q term). Does adding velocity-only process noise
`Q = diag([0, 0, σv², σv²])` empirically improve prediction MSE and innovation
whiteness on a synthetic generator that exhibits true velocity drift?

## Methodology

- Generator: state = [ROI, risk, ROI_vel, risk_vel] with TRUE velocity random walk.
- Two KF variants: Q_zero (production-equivalent, no process noise) vs.
  Q_velocity (Q = diag([0, 0, σv²·1e-6, σv²])).
- T=23 time steps (matching production walk-forward period count).
- Per-seed: prediction MSE + per-objective lag-1 innovation autocorrelation.

## Results — sweep over σv (true velocity drift std-dev)

| σv (true) | Q-zero MSE | Q-vel MSE | Δ MSE | Q-zero lag1 risk | Q-vel lag1 risk | Δ lag1 risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0.0010 | 4.2821e-03 | 4.2820e-03 | -0.0% | -0.135 | -0.136 | +0.001 |
| 0.0050 | 5.0087e-03 | 4.7410e-03 | -5.3% | -0.063 | -0.101 | +0.038 |
| 0.0100 | 7.3510e-03 | 5.4414e-03 | -26.0% | +0.089 | -0.047 | -0.042 |
| 0.0500 | 8.3454e-02 | 1.1433e-02 | -86.3% | +0.657 | +0.149 | -0.508 |
| 0.1000 | 3.2199e-01 | 2.1552e-02 | -93.3% | +0.765 | +0.182 | -0.583 |

## Verdict (at midpoint σv)

- σv (true) = 0.0100
- Q-zero (production-equivalent): MSE = 7.3510e-03, |lag-1 autocorr| risk = 0.089
- Q-velocity (Q = diag([0,0,σv²·1e-6, σv²])): MSE = 5.4414e-03, |lag-1 autocorr| risk = 0.047

🟢 **Q-injection HELPS**: MSE reduced by >5% AND innovations are now WHITE.
Adding Q = diag([0, 0, σv²·1e-6, σv²]) to production KF is a strong candidate
intervention. Requires regression test that the +7.50% FTSE breakthrough is
preserved (recommended: gate behind W22_NC32_VELOCITY_Q env var; smoke 5-seed).

## Caveat

- Synthetic generator may not match FTSE's actual dynamics. Real validation would
  require adding optional Q parameter to `kalman_filter.py` and running an opt-in
  5-seed FTSE smoke. NOT done in this autonomous probe.
- The optimal Q is data-dependent; this experiment only tests Q proportional to
  the true (known) generator σv. Real-data σv would need to be estimated (e.g., via
  Probe AF Mehra residual identification on production logs).
